topSignals.title: Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation

emotionalSignals.title: Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation

contrarianTakes:
```json
{
  "contrarian_takes": [
    {
      "observation": "The mainstream narrative focuses on combating 'disinformation' and 'fake news' by empowering centralized fact-checkers, AI detection tools, or platform content moderation, assuming that identifying and removing false content will restore trust.",
      "hidden_assumption": "That the primary problem is the veracity of information (truth vs. falsehood), and that a neutral, authoritative arbiter can reliably separate the two, or that bad actors simply try to 'trick' people with obviously false data.",
      "structural_flaw": "Astroturfing, as the trend signal suggests, isn't primarily about spreading 'false' information, but about creating *simulated authenticity* and *engineered consensus*. It weaponizes technically 'true' or plausible narratives,
